steps.title: Focus on relative number
steps.content: The sentence claims guilds were more numerous relative to population in some cities than in others. The completion must compare guild density, not merely total guild counts or total population.

steps.title: Reject incomplete data use
steps.content: Choice A relies on a misleading comparison of Bordeaux and Lyon. Choice B misstates the relationship between Lyon and Paris. Choice D gives only Paris's ratio, so it does not show variation across cities.

steps.title: Compare two ratios
steps.content: Rouen's one guild per 661 inhabitants versus Paris's one guild per 4,511 inhabitants directly shows that guild density varied greatly by city. The answer is C.
